description.task: Write a Java program that uses final variables for constants and demonstrates that attempting to reassign them causes a compile error.
instructions: Run the starter code as-is and verify the output.
In a comment at the bottom of main(), try to reassign MAX_RETRIES = 5 and note the compile error message.
Remove the reassignment line before submitting (or comment it out), so the code compiles.
Submit the running code with your comment about the error.
description.hint: The compile error will be: 'cannot assign a value to final variable MAX_RETRIES'. final prevents ANY reassignment after initialization.
description.expectedOutput:
App: BeaCoder
Max retries: 3
Price: 500.0
Tax (18%): 90.0
Total: 590.0

description.task: Create an Account class with a final method getAccountType(). Show that a SavingsAccount subclass cannot override this method.
description.context: A final method cannot be overridden by any subclass. This is used when a behaviour must remain consistent across all subclasses.
instructions: Attempt to override getAccountType() in SavingsAccount and note the compile error.
Remove the override attempt before submitting.
In a comment, explain when you would use a final method.
description.hint: Compile error for overriding a final method: 'getAccountType() in SavingsAccount cannot override getAccountType() in Account; overridden method is final'.
description.expectedOutput:
Owner: Alice | Type: Generic Account
Owner: Bob | Type: Generic Account
Interest Rate: 4.5%

description.task: Demonstrate a final class by creating a final Utility class and attempting (then removing) a subclass to observe the compile error.
description.context: A final class cannot be extended. Java's String class is final for this reason — it guarantees immutability by preventing subclasses from altering its behaviour.
instructions: Run the starter code as-is and verify all three output lines.
Uncomment the 'class ExtendedUtility extends Utility' line temporarily to observe the compile error, then re-comment it.
In a comment in main(), list two real Java classes that are declared final (hint: String, Integer, Math, System).
Submit the working code with the comment.
description.hint: Real-world examples of final classes in Java: java.lang.String, java.lang.Math, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.System.
description.expectedOutput:
Square of 9: 81
Is 'racecar' a palindrome? true
Is 'hello' a palindrome? false
